rulings.Case Title: DOMENIC J CANNIZZARO et al VS. FRED P RICHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 1. -INTERVENOR ROBERT RICHARD II'S Motion To Disqualify. Intervenor Robert Richard II's Motion to Disqualify. Hearing Required re: is the waiver in the Retainer Letter sufficient informed consent. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: DOMENIC J CANNIZZARO et al VS. FRED P RICHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Intervene And To File A Complaint In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 1. -INTERVENOR ROBERT RICHARD II'S Motion To Intervene And To File A Complaint In Intervention. is GRANTED. Robert Richard II has an interest in the outcome of this ligation. Motion is timely in light of representations made by his former counsel Dominic Cannizzaro. Intervenor shall file and serve his motion in intervention forthwith. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN MAYO et al VS. CADELINIA & LEUNG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Def Chicago Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Pltff'S Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 2. -PLAINTIFFS SUSAN MAYO, ELEXANDRIA DIETZ, ROBERT WEISS, PATRICIA WEISS, MEGAN FAULKNER, SIRR LESS, TIMOTHY DIETZ AND JAMES DZIURAS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Defendant Chicago Title Insurance Company's Demurrer To Plaintiff's third Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' Motion for Reconsideration is continued to October 2, 2013 to be heard by Honorable Ronald E. Quidachay. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL WOODY et al VS. CADELINIA & LEUNG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Def Chicago Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Pltff'S Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 3. -PLAINTIFFS DANIEL WOODY, VORNG TYRON, ROXANA GUARDIA, UNA TRAYNOR, RONELLE LEE AND DIANE LOPEZS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Court's Order Sustaining Defendant Chicago Title Insurance Company's Demurrer To Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' Motion for Reconsideration Plaintiffs' Motion for Reconsideration is continued to October 2, 2013 to be heard by Honorable Ronald E. Quidachay.= (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE HORNSBY VS. NEW CENTURY MORTGAGE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses From Pltf And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Agains Pltf In The Amount Of $1187.50
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 4. -DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TRUSTEE'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff And Request For Monetary Sanctions Agains Plaintiff In The Amount Of $1187.50. Defendant's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses from Plaintiff and Request for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: (1) Requests for Admissions are deemed admitted; (2) Plaintiff shall provide responses to Special Interrogatories -Set One, and Form Interrogatories-Set One, without objections to be received by Defendant's Counsel no later than September 30, 2013; (3) Plaintiff shall pay Defendant's counsel $470 within 10 days of Notice of Entry of this Order. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID D CANON VS. NORRIS DAN WALTER DELANY AKA DAN W. DELANY AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft To Respond To Discovery Adn To Procude Documents Pursuant To Dissocveyr Requests (Set One) And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 5.-PLAINTIFF DAVID CANON'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Respond To Discovery And To Procude Documents Pursuant To Dissocvey Requests (Set One) And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant to Respond to Discovery and for Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: (1) Defendant shall provide responses to outstanding discovery, without objections, to be received by Plaintiff's counsel no later than October 4, 2013; (2) Defendant and his counsel shall pay Plaintiff's counsel $1,160 within 10 days of Notice of Entry of Order. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: NINA ZHIGADLO VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 6. -DEFENDANT HYDE STREET HOLDING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Hyde Street Holding Company, LLC's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is continued to September 27, 2013 to be heard together with Meridian Management Group's Motion to Strike. Moving party and its attorney are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LABARTHE et al VS. REBECCA TURNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 7.-DEFENDANTS WESLEY TURNER AND WILLIAM TURNERS' Motion For Sanctions. Defendants' Motion for Sanctions is DENIED. CCP 128.5 is inapplicable. Defendants failed to sustain burden under CCP 128.7(c)(1). = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LABARTHE et al VS. REBECCA TURNER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 7.-DEFENDANTS WESLEY TURNER AND WILLIAM TURNERS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges cause of action for partition. Moving party and their attorney are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: GROSS BELSKY ALONZO LLP VS. ANDREW DIMITRIOU et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 8. -DEFENDANTS ANDREW DIMITRIOU AND DIMITRIOU & ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiff to allege all elements of each cause of action. Default entered on July 25, 2013 was set aside by the Court. Plaintiff's untimely unauthorized Supplemental Opposition is stricken. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SOLLNER VS. NORTH BEACH PARTNERS LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Innocenti'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Cost
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 9. -Defendant Innocenti's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Cost. Defendant Innocenti's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Cost is Continued to October 1, 2013. Reply may be filed in accordance with new hearing date. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: MOUNIR KARDOSH VS. JOHN HAYES et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 10. -DEFENDANT JOHN HAYES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is continued to September 20, 2013 Counsel for the moving party is ordered again (See June 10, 2013 Minute Order CUD-13-645231) to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to Department 501 no later than September 17, 2013 by 4 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: NENG BING DOH VS. KEVIN EPPS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Def, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Def
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, line 11. -PLAINTIFF NENG DOH'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery, to Establish Admissions and Request for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED, subject to opposition as follows: (1) Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions are deemed admitted; (2) Defendant shall provide responses to outstanding discovery, without objections, to be received by Plaintiff's counsel no later than September 23, 2013; (2) Defendant shall pay Plaintiff's counsel $610 within 10 days of Notice of Entry of Order. = (501/PJB)
